introSection: Jet Stream was a steel roller coaster which operated at three amusement parks in the United Kingdom.

sections.title: History
sections.content: Jet Stream was new purchased new in 1974 and operated that year at Battersea Fun Fair. After the park closed, it was sent to Belle Vue Park where it operated from 1975 to 1977. It was again relocated, this time to Ocean Beach Fun Fair where it opened in 1980. The ride closed with the park on 2 September 2007. It was immediately torn down and scrapped.

sections.title: Colour scheme
sections.content: Red track and white supports. The supports were previously painted yellow.

sections.title: External links

fullText:
Jet Stream was a steel roller coaster which operated at three amusement parks in the United Kingdom.

Jet Stream was new purchased new in 1974 and operated that year at Battersea Fun Fair. After the park closed, it was sent to Belle Vue Park where it operated from 1975 to 1977. It was again relocated, this time to Ocean Beach Fun Fair where it opened in 1980. The ride closed with the park on 2 September 2007. It was immediately torn down and scrapped.



Red track and white supports. The supports were previously painted yellow.




 * Jet Stream on the Fairground Archive
